details.body:
finding_summary: The new prompt tells the model to "assign priority using P1 ... P2 ... P3" at `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:341`, but the MilestoneBundle contract expects `priority` to be a numeric field (`1=P1, 2=P2, ...`) at `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1144-1146`. That is conflicting guidance for the same field and can drive schema-invalid milestone bundles on backends that do not hard-coerce structured output.
affected_files: ["src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs"]
remediation_hint: Reword the prompt to request numeric priority values (`1`, `2`, `3`) while explaining their P1/P2/P3 meaning.

finding_summary: The new `validate_dependency_graph()` helper is not actually safe on malformed bundles. It calls `dependency_graph_analysis()` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:838-949`), which canonicalizes IDs without running full validation (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1022-1046`), and `dependency_graph_analysis_from_canonical()` then inserts bead IDs into a `BTreeMap` with silent overwrite semantics (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:714-717`). If two beads normalize to the same ID, the later one wins and the helper can return `Ok(())` or compute the graph against the wrong node instead of reporting the malformed input.
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs"]
remediation_hint: Base `validate_dependency_graph()` on `validated_canonical_bundle()` or explicitly reject duplicate normalized bead IDs before building `bead_index`.

finding_summary: `infer_workstream_order` silently fabricates a total workstream ordering when the workstream-level dependency graph is cyclic even though the bead graph is still a valid DAG. In [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:888](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:888) through [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:945](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:945), any workstreams left with nonzero indegree after the topo walk are appended in input order at lines 939-942. `render_plan_md_checked()` then publishes that sequence as the dependency-derived `Workstream order` at [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1184](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1184) and [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1308](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1308). A valid interleaved plan such as `B1 -> A1 -> A2 -> B2` creates edges `B -> A` and `A -> B`, so the rendered order becomes arbitrary and can contradict the actual sequencing constraints instead of surfacing that there is no single workstream topo order.

finding_summary: `summarize_bundle` can overstate acceptance coverage because it trusts `acceptance_map.covered_by` instead of deriving coverage from the beads or requiring full validation. `canonicalize_bundle()` only backfills `covered_by` when it is empty at [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1064](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1064) through [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1083](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1083), but `summarize_bundle()` counts every non-empty `covered_by` entry as covered at [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:953](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:953) through [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:967](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:967). That means a malformed bundle with stale or unknown `covered_by` references can report 100% acceptance coverage even when no bead actually advances those criteria.

finding_summary: The new milestone-bundle contract is still only enforced by prompt text; domain validation continues to accept underspecified bead proposals and legacy one-way AC mappings.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs
evidence:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:141-145 only rejects an empty `title`; there is still no check that each bead has the newly required `description`, `bead_type`, `priority`, or non-empty `labels`, and no range check that `priority` stays within the new 1..=3 guidance.
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1099-1106 still silently backfills empty `acceptance_map[].covered_by`, so a planner output that omits the bidirectional mapping entirely is treated as valid.
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs:372-377 accepts any milestone bundle that passes `p.validate()`, so these omissions still make it all the way through the pipeline.
remediation_hint: Tighten milestone-bundle validation for newly generated outputs so bead descriptions/type/priority/labels are required, priority is constrained to the supported values, and `acceptance_map[].covered_by` must be explicitly present instead of relying on legacy backfill.

finding_summary: The prompt now asks for `depends_on` and `covered_by` bead IDs, but it never tells the model to assign a stable `bead_id` to each bead proposal.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
evidence:
- src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:337-349 describes titles, descriptions, priorities, labels, dependencies, and AC coverage, but there is no instruction to emit a `bead_id` per bead.
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1052-1065 only synthesizes implicit `bead-N` identifiers after parsing, so an LLM response without explicit IDs has to guess hidden slot numbers to make `depends_on` / `covered_by` line up.
remediation_hint: Update the milestone-bundle template to require a stable `bead_id` on every bead proposal and to reuse those same IDs everywhere cross-references appear.

finding_summary: `validate_dependency_graph()` does not reliably report graph problems on partially invalid bundles, because it is layered on top of full bundle validation and graph analysis is skipped once any earlier error exists.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
evidence:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:963-965 implements `validate_dependency_graph()` by calling `validated_canonical_bundle()`.
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1041-1043 shows `validated_canonical_bundle()` calling `bundle.validate()` first.
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:273-325 only invokes `dependency_graph_analysis_from_canonical()` inside `if errors.is_empty()`, so an unrelated metadata or AC error suppresses the cycle / missing-target diagnostics that this helper is supposed to surface.
remediation_hint: Make `validate_dependency_graph()` canonicalize references and run graph checks independently, or have it return graph diagnostics alongside non-graph validation failures.

finding_summary: `MilestoneBundle::validate()` is now backwards-incompatible with previously accepted milestone payloads. The new hard requirements for bead `description`, `bead_type`, `priority`, `labels`, and non-empty `acceptance_map.covered_by` at `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:146`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:155`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:164`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:171`, and `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:279`, combined with `canonicalize_bundle()` no longer rebuilding empty `covered_by` entries in `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1155`, mean older planner payloads now deserialize successfully but fail runtime validation. That breaks real workflows because completed requirements runs persist the raw planner bundle at `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs:1829` and revalidate it during handoff at `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs:2358`, so an already-completed run can become impossible to hand off or materialize after upgrade.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs
remediation_hint: Keep the stricter prompt guidance, but preserve legacy compatibility by backfilling missing `covered_by` and tolerating absent legacy bead metadata during load/canonicalization, or add an explicit migration path before `validate()` rejects older bundles.

finding_summary: Compatibility validation now rejects any bead priority outside 1..=3, even when callers explicitly use legacy-compatible `validate()`. That is a behavioral regression for previously persisted milestone bundles: old plans that used `priority: 0`/`P0` or backlog priorities will now fail handoff/materialization, even though this change was supposed to be additive and compatibility mode exists precisely to keep legacy bundles loadable.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs
file_refs:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:173-181
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs:2358-2385
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1360-1364
remediation_hint: Apply the `1..=3` restriction only in `BundleValidationMode::Generated`, or widen compatibility mode to accept the historical priority range so legacy bundles still validate while fresh planner output remains constrained.

finding_summary: The new strict planner contract still allows the model to omit `bead_id`, then silently substitutes positional `bead-N` identifiers during validation/canonicalization. Because `RequirementsContract::milestone_bundle()` treats `validate_generated()` as authoritative, planner output can pass without the stable IDs the prompt now requires, leaving dependency and coverage references tied to bead ordering instead of durable identifiers.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs
file_refs:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:192-236
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1179-1203
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs:372-376
remediation_hint: In generated mode, require `bead_id` to be present and non-empty for every bead, and add a contract test that proves `bead_id: null` is rejected.

finding_summary: `validate_generated()` never requires a generated bead to map to at least one acceptance criterion; it only validates entries if the list is present. That means the strict contract still accepts untraceable beads that contribute to no milestone acceptance criterion, even though this bead added bidirectional bead↔AC mapping as a core requirement.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs
file_refs:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:240-286
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs:372-376
remediation_hint: In generated mode, reject empty `bead.acceptance_criteria` lists unless the schema gains an explicit way to represent intentionally non-AC work; add a contract regression test for that omission.

finding_summary: Generated milestone bundles can still omit every workstream description and pass contract validation. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:141-149`, generated-mode validation only checks `workstreams[].name` and `workstreams[].beads`; it never requires a non-empty `workstreams[].description`, even though the new prompt explicitly says each workstream must include a description covering theme, sequencing intent, and deferred scope (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:335-350`). Because `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs:372-377` now relies on `validate_generated()` for planner output, a bundle with `description: null` on every workstream will still be accepted and rendered without the rationale/deferred context this bead is supposed to generate. affected_files: `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs`, `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs`. remediation_hint: In generated mode, reject missing or whitespace-only `workstreams[].description` values and add a contract test that proves omitted descriptions fail milestone-bundle evaluation.

finding_summary: The new deferred-items renderer can miss prompt-compliant deferred notes because its keyword matcher does not recognize the wording the prompt now asks for. The prompt tells the planner to mention deferred scope as "follow-up work" or work that is "not part of this milestone" (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:336,350`), but `contains_deferred_note()` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1016-1030` only matches `defer`, `not included`, `out of scope`, `future work`, `later phase`, `follow-up milestone`, and `intentionally excluded`. A bundle can therefore contain the requested deferred notes while `render_plan_md()` produces no `## Deferred Items` section at all. affected_files: `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs`. remediation_hint: Expand the detector to include the prompt phrases (at minimum `follow-up work` and `not part of this milestone`) or move deferred scope into structured data instead of inferring it from ad hoc text matching.

finding_summary: `validate_dependency_graph()` does not report cycles when the same bundle also has a missing dependency or duplicate bead id. `dependency_graph_analysis_from_nodes()` returns immediately once it sees duplicate/missing-target errors (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:889-912`), so `validate_dependency_graph()` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1079-1083`) never reaches cycle detection for mixed-error graphs. A bundle like `A -> B -> A` plus `C -> missing` will only surface the missing target, which falls short of the helper's contract to check cycles and missing targets together.
affected_files: src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
remediation_hint: collect duplicate/missing-target diagnostics first, then run cycle detection over the resolvable portion of the graph and merge both classes of errors into the returned list.

finding_summary: The new `Deferred Items` section is driven by a small hard-coded phrase list in `contains_deferred_note()` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1025-1040`) and is only emitted when `collect_deferred_items()` finds one of those exact substrings (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1043-1067`, `1379-1383`, `1547-1553`). Because the planner prompt does not constrain the wording of deferred-scope notes, common phrasings like `postponed until beta`, `subsequent milestone`, or `not in scope for alpha` will not be recognized, so `render_plan_md()` can omit the required deferred-work summary even when the workstream/bead description clearly contains deferred scope.
affected_files: src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
remediation_hint: either model deferred work structurally in the bundle, or broaden normalization/matching so the renderer is not dependent on a narrow set of literal phrases.
